en: like we were taught.
And what happened?
That night, the guys in my squad threw a blanket over me,
ko: 그날 밤, 내 팀원들이 나에게 담요를 덮고,

en: took turns punching me in the arm for five minutes,
ko: 차례로 내 팔을 다섯 분 동안 때렸어.

en: and then they poured glue on my hands.
And it worked, too, because I ain't never dropped my weapon since.
ko: 그게 효과가 있었어, 그 이후로 나는 무기를 떨어뜨린 적이 없어.

en: Was Private Santiago ever late for platoon meetings?
ko: 상병 산티아고는 소대 회의에 늦은 적이 있었나?

en: Yes, sir. Was his barracks ever in disorder?
ko: 예, 그렇습니다. 그의 막사는 항상 어질러져 있었나?

en: Yes, sir.
Did he ever fall back on a run? All the time, sir.
ko: 그는 달리기에서 뒤처진 적이 있었나? 항상 그랬습니다.

en: Did he ever, prior to the night of September 6th,
receive a code red?
No, sir. Never?
No, sir.
You got a code red because your palms were sweaty.
ko: 너는 손바닥이 땀으로 젖어서 코드 레드를 받았어.

en: Why didn't Santiago, this burden to his unit, ever get one?
ko: 왜 산티아고, 그의 부대에 짐이 된 그가 코드 레드를 받지 않았지?

en: Dawson wouldn't allow it, sir.
Dawson wouldn't allow it.
The guys talked tough about Santiago, but they wouldn't go near him.
ko: 팀원들은 산티아고에 대해 강하게 말했지만, 그에게 다가가지 않았어.

en: They were too afraid of Dawson, sir.
Object, the witness is speculating.
I'll rephrase, Jeffrey, did you ever want
ko: 다시 말하겠습니다, 제프리, 너는 산티아고에게 코드 레드를 주고 싶었던 적이 있나?

en: to give Santiago a code red?
